task_completion.description: Verify the document uses the Avery 5160 label layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a document page divided into a grid of 3 columns and 10 rows (30 total label cells per page).

task_completion.description: Verify the correct company name and address are used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Summit Peak Adventures' on the first line, '1400 Mountain View Blvd, Suite 200' on the second line, and 'Denver, CO 80202' on the third line within the labels.

task_completion.description: Verify that a full sheet of labels was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the address text populated across all 30 cells in the 3x10 grid, rather than just a single label being filled out.

task_completion.description: Verify the text is center-aligned and the first line is bold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text being centered horizontally within each label cell. The first line ('Summit Peak Adventures') must be visibly thicker/bolder than the rest of the address.

task_completion.description: Verify the font is set to Arial 10pt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the font settings in the application's ribbon/toolbar to confirm 'Arial' and '10' are selected, or visually confirm the text is a standard sans-serif font at a relatively small size.

task_completion.description: Verify the document is saved with the correct name and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the application title bar displaying 'ShippingLabels.docx' or a 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ShippingLabels.docx'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a GUI word processor to create the labels.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for an active word processing application (like Microsoft Word or LibreOffice Writer) being used, rather than a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the labels are formatted using proper application features.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a 'Labels' dialog (e.g., Mailings -> Labels) or a proper table grid, rather than manually typing spaces or tabs to fake a column layout.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use scripts to generate the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that no Python scripts, PowerShell windows, or command-line tools were used to programmatically create the .docx file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Avery 5160 template creates exactly 30 labels per sheet, arranged in a 3 columns by 10 rows grid. The required font size is 10pt (ignore any metadata claiming 20pt). The company name 'Summit Peak Adventures' must be bolded, and all text must be center-aligned. The final file must be named 'ShippingLabels.docx' and saved in C:\Users\Docker\Documents.
